name: Heart Failure - Medication Query (GDMT)
description: Tests ability to find heart failure patients via guideline-directed medical therapy (GDMT) prescriptions. The PheKB HF algorithm captures medications including ACE-I, ARB, beta blockers, and calcium channel blockers. Modern HFrEF GDMT consists of four pillars: ARNi (sacubitril/valsartan), beta blocker, MRA (spironolactone), and SGLT2 inhibitor — plus loop diuretics for symptom control. Synthea distributes patients across these classes. An LLM querying only one drug class (e.g., furosemide) will miss patients managed primarily on other GDMT pillars.
prompt: Find patients prescribed medications used to treat heart failure, including loop diuretics like furosemide, beta blockers like carvedilol, mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists like spironolactone, ARNi combinations such as sacubitril/valsartan, and SGLT2 inhibitors like empagliflozin.

prompts.naive: Find patients on heart failure medications.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed any of the guideline-directed heart failure therapies: loop diuretics (furosemide), beta blockers used for HF (carvedilol), mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists (spironolactone), ARNi (sacubitril/valsartan), or SGLT2 inhibitors (empagliflozin).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any of the four pillars of HFrEF GDMT plus loop diuretics for congestion. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 4603 (furosemide), 20352 (carvedilol), 9997 (spironolactone), 1656339 (sacubitril/valsartan combination), 1545653 (empagliflozin). Combine them in a single comma-separated code search.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with HF medication orders (Paths A and C). Captures both diagnosed patients on GDMT and Path C tricky patients on GDMT-class drugs without a formal HF diagnosis (common with diuretics, beta blockers used for hypertension or arrhythmia, etc).
